functional_requirements.title: Create Order
functional_requirements.description: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Checks user existence, product stock, calculates total price, deducts stock, and creates order with status pending.

functional_requirements.title: Pay Order
functional_requirements.description: User pays for an order. Checks order exists and status is pending, checks user balance, deducts balance, updates order status to paid.

functional_requirements.title: Ship Order
functional_requirements.description: Ship an order. Checks order exists and status is paid, updates status to shipped.

functional_requirements.title: Complete Order
functional_requirements.description: Complete an order. Checks order exists and status is shipped, updates status to completed.

functional_requirements.title: Cancel Order
functional_requirements.description: Cancel an order. Checks order exists and status is pending or paid. Restores product stock, refunds balance if paid, updates status to cancelled.

functional_requirements.title: List Orders
functional_requirements.description: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Displays order details including items, total price, status, and creation time.

functional_requirements.title: Get User Orders
functional_requirements.description: Get all orders for a specific user. Computes total order amount and counts per status.

functional_requirements.title: List Products
functional_requirements.description: List products with info and current stock. Can filter low stock items.

non_functional_requirements.description: Balance and stock must maintain consistency across operations (deduction and restoration).

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order successfully when user exists and stock sufficient
Create order fails with appropriate error when user does not exist
Create order fails with appropriate error when stock insufficient
Pay order succeeds when order is pending and balance sufficient
Pay order fails when order not pending
Pay order fails when balance insufficient
Ship order succeeds when order is paid
Complete order succeeds when order is shipped
Cancel order succeeds when order is pending or paid, restores stock and refunds balance if paid
Cancel order fails when order is shipped or completed
List orders returns correct orders with filters
Get user orders returns correct statistics
List products returns correct product list with optional low stock filter

storage.details: Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders

glossary.order: A record of a purchase transaction containing user, items, total price, status, and creation time.
glossary.pending: Initial order status after creation, awaiting payment.
glossary.completed: Final order status after delivery completion.
glossary.cancelled: Order status when cancelled, with resources restored.
glossary.balance: User's account balance used for payments.
glossary.stock: Available quantity of a product in inventory.

input_spec.description: Input is a JSON object with command and order_data fields, passed via manage_order function.

schema.command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)
schema.order_data: object containing relevant fields: user_id (int), order_id (int), items (list of {product_id, quantity}), status_filter (string), user_filter (int), low_stock (bool)

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.

schema.data: object containing relevant data (e.g., order_id, total_price, new_balance, orders list, products list)
